preferred_descriptors: taut citrus-driven tension with mouthwatering acidity
light-footed fruit-forward reds with a savoury undercurrent
gentle oxidative nuttiness balanced by crisp freshness
zippy pétillant-naturel texture with wild fermentation character
lean mineral whites with a laser-sharp finish
strawberry-bright easy-drinking reds with no heavy extraction
value-driven precision over opulence and weight
avoided_styles: over-extracted high-alcohol reds that heat the finish
overly lean or austere sparkling wines without roundness
overtly sweet or fruit-bomby styles lacking acidity
_synthesized.style_summary: A precision-first palate chasing vibrant acidity, light-to-medium body, and honest fermentation character over extraction, oak, or sheer fruit weight.
_synthesized.palate_persona: This buyer is a high-acidity hunter who prizes tension and freshness above all else — they reach for laser-sharp Rieslings, mineral-edged Chenin Blancs, and Brut Zéro bubbles with oxidative intrigue, consistently rewarding wines that feel alive and precise on the palate. They are drawn to light-bodied, fruit-forward reds — think pét-nat Pinotage, easy Frappato, and Gamay — that seduce through charm and drinkability rather than structure or power, and they will actively call out over-extraction, excess tannin, or alcoholic heat as flaws. Oak is viewed with suspicion: the explicit preference for "something more Chablis-esque" over a lightly oaked Chardonnay, and the appetite for Brut Zéro and Brut Nature sparkling wines, signals someone who wants fermentation purity and stony minerality, not cream and vanilla.
